text: This PR addresses 46 performance audit findings across 30+ frontend components. The majority of fixes are well-executed: granular watch() calls, transform: scaleX for GPU-composited animations, dynamic imports for lazy-loading, useMemo for date formatting and chart data, transition-opacity instead of transition-all, and extraction of inline components. Two regressions were found: a substring-based role check in ProtectedRoute that weakens authorization (important), and an unsafe type assertion in EventSeriesForm (minor). Overall the PR is close to merge-ready with minor corrections needed.

reasoning: The ProtectedRoute authorization change uses string.includes() instead of array.includes(), allowing ADMIN users to pass ORGANIZATION_ADMIN route guards due to substring matching. This is a correctness regression in role-based access control.

message: Replacing blanket watch() with per-field watch('frequency'), watch('daysOfWeek'), etc. correctly fixes the useMemo invalidation issue and prevents unnecessary re-renders across all form field changes.

Extracting the inline ThemeToggleButton function component to an imported ThemeToggle eliminates the React reconciliation problem where a new component type was created on every render, causing unmount/remount of the toggle button.

Correctly fixes a stale closure bug by switching from reviewMutation.variables?.action (closure reference) to variables.action (onSuccess parameter), ensuring the toast message reflects the actual completed mutation.

rolesKey.includes(user.role) performs substring matching instead of exact role comparison. With allowedRoles=['ORGANIZATION_ADMIN'], rolesKey='ORGANIZATION_ADMIN', and user.role='ADMIN', the check would incorrectly return true since 'ADMIN' is a substring of 'ORGANIZATION_ADMIN', granting unauthorized access.
suggestion: Keep allowedRoles.includes(user.role) for the actual membership check but use rolesKey only as the useEffect dependency. The closure captures the latest allowedRoles when the component re-renders, and changing allowedRoles changes rolesKey which triggers the effect: const rolesKey = allowedRoles.join(','); useEffect(() => { if (!allowedRoles.includes(user.role)) ... }, [user, isLoading, rolesKey, router]);

message: Unsafe as EventSeriesFormData type assertion on a partial object. The object passed to calculatePreviewDates contains only 7 fields (frequency, daysOfWeek, etc.) but EventSeriesFormData likely has many more required fields (title, startTime, endTime, isVirtual, capacity, venue, meetingLink). The cast bypasses TypeScript entirely.
suggestion: Accept Partial<EventSeriesFormData> in calculatePreviewDates or define a narrow interface with only the fields the function actually needs.

message: React.ElementType is overly broad - accepts intrinsic elements like 'div' or 'span' which would fail at runtime when rendered as <IconComponent />.
suggestion: Use React.ComponentType<{ className?: string }> instead: const IconComponent: React.ComponentType<{ className?: string }> = BADGE_ICONS[name] ?? Award;
